place: Lake Mohawk CDP, New Jersey, New Jersey

facts.text: Lake Mohawk was named after the Mohawk tribe and Princess White Deer, a Mohawk performer who attended the opening ceremonies.
The community was built to resemble an alpine village, with Lake Mohawk Tudor homes blending English Cottage, Tudor, and German Baronial styles.
Lake Mohawk is the largest artificial private lake in New Jersey, created by damming a tributary of the Wallkill River in 1926.
An Olympic‑size pool called the Marine Pool, designed to look like an ocean liner deck, hosts swimming and diving events.
The town center’s buildings resemble little castles, echoing the alpine motif that characterizes the entire community.
The community straddles Sparta and Byram townships, so residents receive services from either township depending on their location.
